title: DSDS Token Overrides Definition
description: The shared token-override map used by anatomy parts, variant values, and states. Keys are token-purpose names that say what visual attribute the token controls; values are entity references to the token that supplies the value. One definition anchors the same rule in every position: token references MUST name a documented token, never a raw value, so styling can't silently drift from the token source.

tokenOverrides.description: Design token overrides for one part, variant value, or state. Keys are token-purpose names describing the visual attribute the token controls (e.g., 'background', 'text-color', 'border-radius', 'padding-horizontal'). Values are token `identifier` references (e.g., 'color-action-primary', 'radius-md') and follow the same resolution rule as every entity reference: in a system that documents its token layer, they MUST resolve to a documented token. Validators resolve them when a token layer is present (see entityRef resolution). Raw values (e.g., '#ffffff', '4px') are not valid here; token-less systems document raw values in design-specifications, not in token-override maps.
